leadText: Tachyon Cutter (Japanese: タキオンカッター Tachyon Cutter) is a damage-dealing Steel-type multistrike move introduced during Generation IX, in The Indigo Disk. It is the signature move of Iron Crown.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Tachyon Cutter inflicts damage, hitting twice per use. Each strike has an equal chance to be a critical hit . It bypasses accuracy checks to always hit, unless the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of a move such as Dig or Fly . If used by a Pokémon with Sharpness , its power is increased by 50%. Tachyon Cutter will hit again if the first strike breaks a substitute. Mirror Coat will only acknowledge the second strike of this move. If a Focus Sash activates before the last strike is dealt, the following strike will cause the defending Pokémon to faint. A Focus Band can still activate repeatedly to prevent fainting, but each chance is independent. If the user is holding a King's Rock or Razor Fang , each consecutive hit has an equal chance to cause the opponent to flinch . If the target has Stamina , each strike will activate it.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S V The user attacks by launching particle blades at the target twice in a row. This attack never misses.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Tachyon Cutter is the only multistrike move that cannot miss under normal conditions. A tachyon is a hypothetical particle that can move at superluminal (faster than light) speeds.
